channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bird Flu Crisis: A Growing Global Health Concern
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing threat of bird flu, highlighting its rapid spread among poultry populations and increasing concerns about potential human transmission. Experts warn that the virus is...

item.title: Trump Requests Review of Balogun's Red Card Decision
item.description: President Donald Trump confirmed Monday he asked FIFA president Gianni Infantino to review the red card decision against U.S. striker Folarin Balogun, stating he did not request the penalty to be over...

item.title: Transport Operators Threaten Strike Over Fines and Registration Costs
item.description: A group of transport operators in Mombasa has issued a seven-day strike notice to the county government, demanding the suspension of the National Transport and Safety Authority's (NTSA) Instant Fines ...

item.title: EMPD Officer and Civilian Charged in R14m Stones Theft Case
item.description: A joint operation by police and the Independent Police Investigative Directorate (Ipid) arrested EMPD officer Adrian Mackenzie and civilian Etienne van der Walt in connection with an alleged R14m prec...

item.title: Gilas Pilipinas suffers fourth straight loss to Australia in FIBA World Cup qualifiers
item.description: Gilas Pilipinas suffered a 92-49 defeat against Australia in the FIBA World Cup 2027 Asian Qualifiers, marking their fourth consecutive loss in Group A. The Philippines, without key player Justin Brow...

item.title: Nigeria Condemns Killings of Citizens in South Africa's Anti-Migrant Protests
item.description: Nigeria has strongly condemned the deaths of two of its nationals in South Africa amid escalating anti-migrant protests. The Foreign Ministry confirmed the killings occurred on June 28, with one victi...

item.title: Tanzania Launches Vision 2050 Reforms to Achieve Trillion-Dollar Economy by 2050
item.description: Tanzania has officially begun implementing its ambitious Vision 2050 development plan, marking a pivotal shift from strategic planning to concrete action as the East African nation works toward buildi...

item.title: Co-op Bank's £300 Switch Incentive and Comparison with Other Banks' Offers
item.description: Co-operative Bank has launched a switch offer paying up to £300 for existing customers switching to its current account, with £200 for new customers. The payment is structured as £125 upfront and £125...

item.title: Trump's Soccer Rule Ignorance Sparks FIFA Dispute Over Player Reinstatement
item.description: The article details President Donald Trump's controversial remarks regarding a U.S. soccer player's reinstatement after a red card incident. During a press availability, Trump admitted he was unaware ...

item.title: Pension Withdrawals for Unemployed Workers: Balancing Immediate Needs with Long-Term Security
item.description: When Babatunde lost his job, he faced financial stress and turned to Nigeria's pension system. He withdrew 25% of his retirement savings after four months of unemployment, as allowed by the Pension Re...

item.title: Comparing Portable and Fixed Air Conditioning: Costs, Efficiency, and Best Use Cases for UK Homes
item.description: The article provides a detailed comparison between portable and fixed air conditioning systems in the context of UK homeowners facing overheating issues. It outlines key factors such as upfront costs,...

item.title: Manipur Violence: Two Assam Rifles Soldiers Killed in Militant Encounter in Ukhrul
item.description: Two Assam Rifles soldiers were killed in a gunfight with unidentified militants in Ukhrul, Manipur, on Monday. The incident occurred in the Tangkhul Naga-majority district, with operations ongoing to ...

item.title: British climber aims to set 16-hour record for climbing Mount Kilimanjaro
item.description: British mountaineer John Rees-Evans is set to attempt a groundbreaking 16-hour record for ascending and descending Mount Kilimanjaro, the world's highest freestanding mountain. The challenge, schedule...

item.title: Chinese Artists Create Hyper-Realistic 3D Icons Inspired by Dieter Rams' Minimalist Design Philosophy
item.description: The article discusses a creative project by Chinese digital artists Lu Yan (elliot9) and Zhiyi Zhang, who reimagined the iconic design language of Dieter Rams for modern digital interfaces. Drawing in...

item.title: Iran mourns Khamenei with anti-US chants during funeral procession
item.description: A massive funeral procession for the late Iranian supreme leader, Ali Khamenei, is underway in Tehran, with mourners expressing strong anti-US and anti-Israel sentiments. The casket, transported throu...

item.title: Man Grieves Over Loss of Chickens, Netizens Commiserate
item.description: A young man is seen in a TikTok video mourning the death of all his chickens, which he had invested significant time and money into raising. The video shows him sitting beside the dead birds, which we...

item.title: Philippine tennis star Alex Eala exits Wimbledon after defeat; Italian player Jasmine Paolini reaches quarterfinals
item.description: Filipino tennis sensation Alexandra 'Alex' Eala concluded her Wimbledon campaign after a hard-fought 6-4, 4-6, 6-3 loss to Italy's Jasmine Paolini in the round of 16. The defeat marks the end of Eala'...

item.title: Minister Umahi updates on Sokoto-Badagry Highway progress and addresses Sango-Idiroko road issues
item.description: Minister of Works, Engr. David Umahi, recently inspected the Sokoto–Badagry Superhighway project, praising the progress made on Section III. He highlighted that construction is aligning with engineeri...

item.title: Ontario Liberal leadership race narrows as Cerjanec withdraws
item.description: Rob Cerjanec, representing Ajax in the Ontario Legislative Assembly, has decided to suspend his campaign for the provincial Liberal leadership after consultations with family, supporters, and colleagu...

item.title: White House Accuses Smithsonian Museum of Political Activism in 162-Page Report
item.description: The White House has released a 162-page report accusing the Smithsonian's National Museum of American History of 'extreme political activism,' claiming its leadership has transformed the American narr...
